title: fragaria chiloensis var. sandwicensis
intro: Fragaria chiloensis var. sandwicensis, also known as the Hawaiian strawberry or Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered species of strawberry native to Hawaii. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Fragaria chiloensis var. sandwicensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, although its use is highly regulated due to its endangered status.
